name: pd_incidents
description: List all active PagerDuty incidents (triggered and acknowledged), sorted by urgency. Use this to answer 'what's firing?' or 'what's on fire?' questions. For detailed incident information, use pd_incident_detail instead.

name: pd_incident_detail
description: Get detailed information about a specific incident including timeline, alerts, and notes. Use this after pd_incidents to deep-dive into a specific incident. For a quick overview of all active incidents, use pd_incidents instead.

name: pd_oncall
description: Show current on-call schedules and escalation policies across all schedules. Use this to answer 'who's on call?' or 'who's oncall right now?' questions.

name: pd_services
description: List all PagerDuty services and their current operational status (critical, warning, active, disabled). Use this to check service health or get service IDs for use with pd_recent.

name: pd_recent
description: Show recent incidents for a service or across all services with summary statistics. Use this to answer 'what happened last night?' or investigate incident patterns. Use pd_incidents for currently active incidents instead.

service.description: Filter to a specific PagerDuty service ID

name: pd_incident_ack
description: Acknowledge a triggered incident, stopping further escalation. Requires --confirm flag and PAGERDUTY_EMAIL to be set. Read-only by default; use pd_incidents to view incidents before acknowledging. For resolving incidents, use pd_incident_resolve instead.

incident_id.description: The PagerDuty incident ID to acknowledge (e.g., P123ABC)

confirm.description: Must be set to true to actually acknowledge the incident

name: pd_incident_resolve
description: Resolve an incident, marking it as fixed. Requires --confirm flag and PAGERDUTY_EMAIL to be set. Read-only by default; use pd_incidents to view incidents before resolving. For acknowledging incidents, use pd_incident_ack instead.

incident_id.description: The PagerDuty incident ID to resolve (e.g., P123ABC)

confirm.description: Must be set to true to actually resolve the incident

name: pd_incident_note
description: Add a permanent note to an incident's timeline. Useful for documenting root cause, investigation steps, or remediation actions. Requires --confirm flag and PAGERDUTY_EMAIL to be set. For viewing incidents, use pd_incidents or pd_incident_detail instead.

incident_id.description: The PagerDuty incident ID to add a note to (e.g., P123ABC)

confirm.description: Must be set to true to actually add the note
